response:
To determine the number of bottles Brittany needs, we first calculate the total shampoo usage for a leap year, which has 366 days. Since she washes her hair every other day, she will use shampoo approximately 183 times, consuming a total of 45.75 ounces when multiplying the frequency by the 1/4 ounce per use. This calculation assumes she starts on the first day and continues consistently throughout the year [start_date].

Dividing the total consumption of 45.75 ounces by the 14-ounce bottle size reveals that she requires roughly 3.27 bottles to cover the entire period. Because she cannot purchase a fraction of a bottle, she must round up to the next whole number, meaning she needs to buy 4 bottles in total. This ensures she has enough product to last until the very end of the year [end_date].
